channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rutland residents call for more equitable patrols amid rising crime and safety concerns
item.description: A public meeting at Rutland Centennial Hall in Kelowna, British Columbia, drew roughly 200 residents to discuss growing concerns about crime and safety. Conservative MLA Gavin Dew hosted the event, no...

item.title: Westville Residents Call for Budget That Addresses Housing, Electricity, and Scholar Transport Needs
item.description: Residents of Westville in Durban made their voices heard during the Standing Committee on Appropriations’ public hearings on the national budget. They stressed that the budget must go beyond technical...

item.title: Government urges strict compliance with mining laws to protect national interests
item.description: The Tanzanian government, kupitia the Mining Commission, has once again emphasised the importance of following laws and regulations in the mining sector. Speaking in Dodoma, the Commissioner of the Mi...

item.title: Spring temperatures cause snowmelt and road issues across Saskatchewan
item.description: As the first day of spring approaches on March 20, 2026, Saskatchewan is experiencing unseasonably warm temperatures, with some cities reaching the high teens Celsius and southwestern areas hitting th...

item.title: Trump references Pearl Harbor in awkward remark during meeting with Japanese Prime Minister
item.description: During a bilateral meeting with Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, President Donald Trump made an ill-received joke about the 1941 Pearl Harbor attack while explaining why the U.S. did not inform...

item.title: Key News Highlights in Nigeria for Friday, 20th March 2026
item.description: Good morning Nigeria! Today, President Bola Tinubu don meet UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer for high-level talks wey dey focused on trade, investment, defence, and culture. One major deal signed na £74...

item.title: Local gold prices fall as Middle East conflict affects global markets
item.description: Karachi: Gold, traditionally considered a safe haven during global crises, has lost some of its appeal as prices drop sharply both locally and internationally amid the escalating war in the Middle Eas...

item.title: Detainees at Canberra’s Alexander Maconochie Centre earn as little as $1 per hour for prison work
item.description: Detainees at the Alexander Maconochie Centre (AMC), the ACT’s only adult prison, are paid between $36 and just under $72 per week depending on the role and hours of work, which equates to roughly $1 t...

item.title: True Leadership Means Serving the People, Not Serving Oneself
item.description: As Kenya heads toward the 2027 elections, columnist Caren Wakoli argues that the country does not lack leaders, but lacks leaders who genuinely understand that leadership is service. She observes that...

item.title: Tackling Corruption as the Philippines’ Most Urgent Economic Reform
item.description: In his third open letter to President Ferdinand Marcos Jr., Mon Abrea, known as the 'Philippine Tax Whiz,' warns that corruption acts as the country’s biggest hidden tax, undermining economic progress...

item.title: The historical and economic impact of sugar in KwaZulu-Natal
item.description: Sugar has been more than just a crop in KwaZulu-Natal; it has been the backbone of the province’s economy and the foundation of communities for over 165 years. The industry began with the arrival of i...

item.title: European Cloud Providers Urge EU to Halt Broadcom’s Shutdown of VMware Partner Program
item.description: A European trade group representing smaller cloud infrastructure providers has formally asked the European Commission to intervene in Broadcom’s decision to terminate VMware’s Cloud Service Provider (...

item.title: Government Plans Budget Reforms to Ease Tax Burden for Traders and Industrialists
item.description: Dar es Salaam – Tanzania government has promised to tackle tax challenges in the 2026/27 budget as part of efforts to support traders and industrialists and attract more local and foreign investment. ...

item.title: Remulla rejects viral quote telling public to ignore flood control controversy
item.description: This fact-check article clarifies a viral claim circulating on social media that Interior Secretary Jonvic Remulla allegedly urged Filipinos to move on from the flood control corruption controversy an...

item.title: Njugush and Celestine Ndinda spark speculation after separate gym posts amid ongoing breakup rumours
item.description: Comedian Njugush, whose real name is Timothy Ndegwa Kimani, and his wife Celestine Ndinda, popularly known as Wakavinye, have been trending online for the past few weeks kutokana na rumours za kwamba ...

item.title: Katherine LaNasa Discusses The Pitt's New Episode Addressing Immigration and Trauma
item.description: In a recent interview with The Globe and Mail, veteran actress Katherine LaNasa talks about her role as charge nurse Dana Evans in the HBO Max drama *The Pitt*, which streams on Crave in Canada. The E...

item.title: Comparing Leadership Approaches: How England and India Manage Their Players
item.description: The article contrasts the approaches of the English and Indian cricket management systems through the experiences of players like Liam Livingstone, Ishan Kishan, and Sanju Samson. Liam Livingstone, a ...

item.title: Iran Executes Wrestler Amidst Ongoing Protests and Regional Tensions
item.description: Iran don carry out the execution of 19-year-old Saleh Mohammadi, a rising star in wrestling, alongside two other men, in what human rights groups dey call a terrible injustice. Dis one happen on Thurs...

item.title: New Edition hotel on Lake Como targets modern luxury travellers
item.description: Lake Como has long been associated with glamour, history and elite tourism, attracting notable figures from Leonardo da Vinci through to modern celebrities like Taylor Swift. Its reputation as a luxur...

item.title: Labour divisions deepen over proposed extension to migrant settlement rules
item.description: Labour MPs are increasingly divided over proposed immigration reforms led by Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood, particularly plans to extend the qualifying period for indefinite leave to remain (ILR) fro...
